binaries.title: luafv.sys - Missing synchronization (race) on UAC file-virtualization directory-query stream handle context lazy init, fixed behind a WIL feature-staging gate

changed_functions.name: LuafvQueryStoreDirectory
key_changes: ADDED (feature-gated): Recursive exclusive push lock (FltAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx) acquired at function entry on the stream-handle-context push lock at arg3+0x20, tracking owner thread (KeGetCurrentThread via gs:0x188) at +0x08 and recursion count at +0x10.
ADDED (feature-gated): Matching FltReleasePushLockEx at function exit, with recursion-count decrement and owner-thread clearing when recursion returns to base.

GATE: Both the acquire and the release are guarded by Feature_3757245754__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() (a WIL feature-staging predicate). When it returns 0, the lock is skipped (jz at 0x1C0015F66 / 0x1C00160DC) and the original unsynchronized body runs unchanged. This is a staged / feature-gated rollout with the pre-existing path retained, not an unconditional fix.

UNPATCHED behavior: the lazy allocation of a 0x40-byte PagedPool buffer at arg3+0x60 via LuafvAllocatePool (tag 0x16), memset zeroing, two LuafvOpenDirectory calls, MmMapLockedPagesSpecifyCache on the query MDL, and LuafvMergeStoreDirectory all execute with NO lock.
Body logic is otherwise unchanged; the only functional difference is the feature-gated synchronization wrapper.
changed_functions.security_notes: PRE-PATCH PATTERN: LuafvQueryStoreDirectory is reached only from the pre-operation callback LuafvPreDirectoryControl (0x1C000FD10) for IRP_MJ_DIRECTORY_CONTROL with minor function IRP_MN_QUERY_DIRECTORY (minor==1, guarded by 'cmp byte [Iopb+5],1' at 0x1C000FDB6, then a file-information-class dispatch at 0x1C000FDE0 / jump table at 0x1C0017297; call site at 0x1C000FE03). It lazily allocates and populates a 0x40-byte buffer in the shared stream-handle context (arg3+0x60) and maps the query MDL, all without holding any lock. If two directory-query operations run concurrently on the same stream-handle context (overlapped/async IRP_MJ_DIRECTORY_CONTROL on the same handle), both can observe arg3+0x60==NULL, both allocate, and one store overwrites the other; the loser's 0x40 PagedPool allocation (tag 0x16) is leaked and both threads run the open/merge pipeline over the same shared buffer, corrupting the directory-merge state. No free of arg3+0x60 occurs in this function, so no use-after-free is demonstrated. The patched build wraps the body in FltAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx/FltReleasePushLockEx, but only when the WIL feature Feature_3757245754__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage is enabled. CWE-362 (Missing Synchronization / Race Condition). Severity Low: narrow directory-query-only reachability, first-init window, feature-gated delivery, and no demonstrated memory-safety violation beyond a pool leak / concurrent double-init.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED LuafvQueryStoreDirectory @ 0x1C0014DB4 (no lock):
0x1C0014DC8  mov     rsi, [rdx+10h]          ; Iopb
0x1C0014DCC  mov     rdi, r8                 ; StreamHandleContext
0x1C0014DCF  mov     r8, [r8+60h]            ; read ctx->buf, NO LOCK
0x1C0014DD9  mov     r12d, [rsi+18h]         ; len
0x1C0014DDD  test    r8, r8
0x1C0014DE0  jnz     0x1C0014E12             ; skip alloc if set
0x1C0014DE2  mov     ebx, 40h                ; size 0x40
0x1C0014DE7  mov     r8b, 16h                ; tag 0x16
0x1C0014DEC  lea     ecx, [rbx-3Fh]          ; PoolType 1 (PagedPool)
0x1C0014DEF  call    LuafvAllocatePool       ; NO LOCK
0x1C0014DF4  mov     [rdi+60h], rax          ; store ctx->buf, NO LOCK
0x1C0014DF8  test    rax, rax
0x1C0014DFB  jz      0x1C0018DCE             ; -> 0xC000009A
0x1C0014E09  call    memset
0x1C0014E0E  mov     r8, [rdi+60h]
0x1C0014E1B  call    LuafvOpenDirectory      ; NO LOCK
0x1C0014E2A  mov     r8, [rdi+60h]
0x1C0014E32  add     r8, 20h
0x1C0014E3C  call    LuafvOpenDirectory      ; NO LOCK
0x1C0014E53  mov     rcx, [rsi+40h]          ; query MDL
0x1C0018DFA  call    MmMapLockedPagesSpecifyCache ; NO LOCK
0x1C0014EB5  call    LuafvMergeStoreDirectory ; NO LOCK
0x1C0014ED9  retn

PATCHED LuafvQueryStoreDirectory @ 0x1C0015F34 (feature-gated lock):
0x1C0015F59  call    Feature_3757245754__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C0015F64  test    eax, eax
0x1C0015F66  jz      0x1C0015FA3             ; feature OFF -> skip lock, run old path
0x1C0015F68  mov     rbx, [rdi+20h]          ; push lock ctx+0x20
0x1C0015F6C  mov     rax, gs:188h            ; KeGetCurrentThread
0x1C0015F75  cmp     [rbx+8], rax            ; already owner?
0x1C0015F7B  add     [rbx+10h], r14d         ; recursive ++count
0x1C0015F86  call    FltAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx
0x1C0015F9B  mov     [rbx+8], rax            ; owner = current thread
0x1C0015F9F  mov     [rbx+10h], r14d         ; recursion = 1
0x1C0015FA3  ... [body identical, serialized only when feature on] ...
0x1C00160D5  call    Feature_3757245754__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C00160DC  jz      0x1C001610A            ; feature OFF -> skip release
0x1C00160F1  sub     dword ptr [rcx+10h], 1
0x1C00160FE  call    FltReleasePushLockEx

added: wil_details_* / wil_*StagingConfig* / wil_details_FeatureReporting_* (WIL feature-staging plumbing supporting the gate; no independent luafv security logic)

security_findings.entry_point: Pre-operation callback LuafvPreDirectoryControl (0x1C000FD10), registered for IRP_MJ_DIRECTORY_CONTROL. Reached only for minor function IRP_MN_QUERY_DIRECTORY (minor==1) via the store-merge path; call site at 0x1C000FE03.
call_chain: User-mode directory enumeration (FindFirstFileW/FindNextFileW / NtQueryDirectoryFile) on a luafv-virtualized directory by a non-elevated process

LuafvPreDirectoryControl requires minor == IRP_MN_QUERY_DIRECTORY (cmp byte [Iopb+5],1 at 0x1C000FDB6), dispatches on file-information class (cmp edx,3 at 0x1C000FDE0 / jump table at 0x1C0017297), and calls LuafvQueryStoreDirectory at 0x1C000FE03
LuafvQueryStoreDirectory runs the unsynchronized lazy-init/open/merge pipeline on the shared context buffer at arg3+0x60
security_findings.description: LuafvQueryStoreDirectory lazily initializes a 0x40-byte buffer stored in the shared stream-handle context at arg3+0x60: (1) read arg3+0x60, (2) if NULL allocate via LuafvAllocatePool(PagedPool, 0x40, tag 0x16), (3) store at arg3+0x60, (4) memset zero, (5) two LuafvOpenDirectory calls (into buf and buf+0x20), (6) MmMapLockedPagesSpecifyCache on the query MDL from Iopb+0x40, (7) LuafvMergeStoreDirectory merging store and virtual directory listings. In the unpatched build none of these steps hold a lock. Two concurrent directory-query operations on the same stream-handle context can both observe arg3+0x60==NULL, both allocate, and one store overwrites the other, leaking the loser's allocation and causing both threads to run the open/merge pipeline over the same shared buffer, corrupting the directory-merge state. The buffer at arg3+0x60 is not freed within this function, so no use-after-free is demonstrated by the diff. The patched build adds FltAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx/FltReleasePushLockEx around the body, but only when the WIL feature Feature_3757245754__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage is enabled; when disabled the original unsynchronized path runs.
security_findings.impact: Demonstrable impact is limited to a pool-memory leak (orphaned 0x40 PagedPool allocation, tag 0x16) and concurrent double-initialization / corruption of the shared per-context directory-merge buffer under a narrow directory-query race. No information leak, use-after-free, controlled kernel read/write, or privilege-escalation primitive is established by the two builds.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1) Run as a standard (non-elevated) user with UAC file virtualization active. 2) Enumerate a directory backed by the luafv virtual store. 3) From multiple threads (or via overlapped/async I/O) issue concurrent IRP_MJ_DIRECTORY_CONTROL / IRP_MN_QUERY_DIRECTORY requests (NtQueryDirectoryFile / FindNextFileW) on the same handle so two callbacks enter LuafvQueryStoreDirectory on the same context. The race window is between the NULL-check of arg3+0x60 (0x1C0014DCF/0x1C0014DDD) and the store of the new pointer (0x1C0014DF4), widest on the first query when arg3+0x60 is still NULL.
evidence_addresses: 0x1C0014DCF: mov r8, [r8+60h] -- unsynchronized read of shared buffer pointer (no lock in unpatched)
0x1C0014DDD/0x1C0014DE0: test r8,r8 ; jnz -- NULL-check both threads can pass
0x1C0014E2A: mov r8, [rdi+60h] -- second LuafvOpenDirectory re-reads shared pointer
0x1C0014EB5: call LuafvMergeStoreDirectory -- merge over shared buffer

breakpoints.why: Entry. rcx=PFLT_INSTANCE, rdx=PFLT_CALLBACK_DATA, r8=StreamHandleContext. [r8+0x60] is the lazily-allocated buffer being raced.
Unsynchronized read of ctx+0x60. If NULL, the alloc path is taken. Two threads observing NULL here is the race condition.
LuafvAllocatePool(PagedPool, 0x40, 0x16). The store at 0x1C0014DF4 (mov [rdi+60h], rax) is the unsynchronized race-decider.
Second LuafvOpenDirectory re-reads [rdi+0x60]; in a race this may differ from the pointer just allocated.
The pre-op callback. Requires IRP_MN_QUERY_DIRECTORY; calls LuafvQueryStoreDirectory at 0x1C000FE03.

watch: [rdi+0x60] (arg3+0x60): the shared lazy-init buffer pointer -- watch for two threads seeing NULL, or one pointer overwriting another's
rax return of LuafvAllocatePool @ 0x1C0014DEF: the new 0x40 PagedPool buffer (tag 0x16); the loser's is leaked
[rsi+0x40]: query MDL from Iopb used by MmMapLockedPagesSpecifyCache
gs:0x188 (KeGetCurrentThread): used only by the patched feature-on path for recursive lock ownership
r12d = [rsi+0x18]: length parameter passed to LuafvOpenDirectory and LuafvMergeStoreDirectory
key_offsets: 0x1C0014DCF: read of shared buffer pointer (no lock in unpatched)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1) Standard (non-elevated) user with UAC virtualization active. 2) Enumerate a luafv-virtualized directory. 3) Issue concurrent overlapped/async IRP_MJ_DIRECTORY_CONTROL / IRP_MN_QUERY_DIRECTORY (NtQueryDirectoryFile / FindNextFileW) on the same handle so two callbacks enter LuafvQueryStoreDirectory on the same context; the race is widest on the first query when arg3+0x60 is NULL.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Demonstrable: a leaked 0x40 PagedPool allocation (tag 0x16) when both threads allocate, and concurrent open/merge over the same shared buffer corrupting the directory-merge state. On patched builds with the feature enabled, the push lock at 0x1C0015F86 serializes the path; with the feature disabled the patched build behaves like the unpatched one. No use-after-free, arbitrary read/write, or EoP primitive is demonstrable from the diff.
debugging_aids.notes: The lock is at stream_context+0x20 (recursive exclusive push lock: owner thread at +0x08, recursion count at +0x10). It is NOT acquired unconditionally: it is gated by Feature_3757245754__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() (a WIL feature-staging predicate); if it returns 0 (jz at 0x1C0015F66) the lock is skipped and the original path runs. 0xC000009A (STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES) is the allocation-failure return.

summary: The only behavioral change between the two luafv.sys builds is in LuafvQueryStoreDirectory (0x1C0014DB4 -> 0x1C0015F34). The patched build wraps the function body in a recursive exclusive push lock (FltAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx/FltReleasePushLockEx) on the stream-handle-context push lock at arg3+0x20, addressing a missing-synchronization pattern in the lazy initialization of a 0x40-byte per-context buffer at arg3+0x60. The lock is gated behind a WIL feature-staging flag (Feature_3757245754__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage); when the flag is off the lock is skipped and the pre-existing unsynchronized path runs, so this is a staged / feature-gated rollout with the old path retained. The function is reached only from the pre-operation callback LuafvPreDirectoryControl for IRP_MJ_DIRECTORY_CONTROL with minor function IRP_MN_QUERY_DIRECTORY (not from CREATE/READ/WRITE/QUERY_EA/SET_EA). Under concurrent directory-query I/O on the same context, two threads can both allocate the buffer and one overwrites the other, leaking the loser's 0x40 PagedPool allocation (tag 0x16) and causing concurrent double-initialization / corruption of the shared buffer. No free of the buffer occurs in the function, so no use-after-free is demonstrated; there is no evidence for an information leak, controlled read/write, or privilege escalation. Severity Low, CWE-362. All other function-level differences are global-data address relocations caused by linking in the WIL feature-staging framework.
